samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers to store intermediate values and control the multiplication process.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit input signal representing the multiplicand.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit input signal representing the multiplier.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit output signal representing the product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size">4</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        // Extension of input signals
        wire [3:0] mul_a_ext;
        wire [3:0] mul_b_ext;
        
        assign mul_a_ext = {4{1'b0}} | mul_a;
        assign mul_b_ext = {4{1'b0}} | mul_b;
        // Multiplication operation
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1) begin : gen_mul
                wire [3:0] partial_product;
                
                if(mul_b[i]) begin
                    assign partial_product = mul_a << i;
                end else begin
                    assign partial_product = 'd0;
                end
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Add of partial products
        reg [7:0] sum_reg[0:size-1];
        integer j;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum_reg[j] <= 8'd0;
                end
            end else begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum_reg[j] <= sum_reg[j] + partial_product;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Final product calculation
        reg [7:0] mul_out_reg;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= sum_reg[0] + sum_reg[1] + sum_reg[2] + sum_reg[3];
            end
        end
        
        assign mul_out = mul_out_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>
